## NYT::NFlow::TUnitedParameters&lt;NYT::NFlow::TBigRTQueueSource&gt; {#NYT_NFlow_TUnitedParameters_NYT_NFlow_TBigRTQueueSource}
<!-- This file is generated by yt/yt/flow/tools/generate_yson_struct_doc/generate.sh script -->
<!-- Before using doc generation tool check readme: yt/yt/flow/tools/generate_yson_struct_doc/README.md -->
Source: [yt/yt/flow/library/cpp/common/registry-inl.h]({{source-root}}/yt/yt/flow/library/cpp/common/registry-inl.h)

#|
|| **Parameter** | **Description** ||
|| `finite` | **Type**: `bool`
**Default value**: `false`
Treat the source as finite: remember the number of messages in it on startup and transition the stream to the completed state after all those messages are read. ||
|| `queue_path` | **Type**: `NYT::NYPath::TRichYPath`
**Required parameter**
Path to the queue including the cluster. ||
|| `consumer_path` | **Type**: `NYT::NYPath::TRichYPath`
**Required parameter**
Path to the queue consumer with the cluster specified. ||
|| `try_parse_flow_queue_meta` | **Type**: `bool`
**Default value**: `true`
Whether to retrieve and parse meta from the queue (the meta contains watermarks provided by the writer to the queue). ||
|| `flow_queue_meta_column` | **Type**: `std::string`
**Default value**: `flow_queue_meta`
Column from which to retrieve meta. ||
|| `ignore_malformed_flow_queue_meta` | **Type**: `bool`
**Default value**: `false`
Whether to ignore invalid meta or fail. ||
|| `partition_filter` | **Type**: `std::optional<std::vector<std::pair<int, int>>>`
 ||
|| `framing_format` | **Type**: [NYT::NFlow::EFramingFormat](./all_yson_structs#NYT_NFlow_EFramingFormat)
**Default value**: `auto`
 ||
|#


{% cut "**Additional parameters**" %}


#|
|| `update_info_period` | **Type**: [TDuration](./all_yson_structs#TDuration)
**Default value**: `15s`
 ||
|| `byte_size_alpha` | **Type**: `double`
**Default value**: `0.05`
 ||
|| `update_partition_count_period` | **Type**: [TDuration](./all_yson_structs#TDuration)
**Default value**: `1m`
How often the controller should update the partition count in the queue (the controller changes the computation partition count according to the queue partition count). ||
